data.title: LuxExperience B.V GAAP EPS of -€0.06, revenue of €242.5M
data.description:
• LuxExperience B.Vpress release (NYSE:LUXE): Q3 GAAP EPS of -€0.06.
  • Revenue of €242.5M (+3.8% Y/Y).
  • GMV growth of 3.8% to €261.3 million in Q3 FY25 as compared to €251.9 million in the prior year period.
  • Outstanding Average Order Value increasing by +8.8% to €753 LTM in Q3 FY25 vs. Q3 FY24.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of €9.3 million and adjusted EBITDA margin of 3.9% - FYTD 25 Adjusted EBITDA margin at 4.3%.
  • Positive Operating Cash Flow of €18.7 million in Q3 FY25.

data.title: Informatica enters into new strategic agreement with Microsoft
data.description:
• Informatica (NYSE:INFA) has announced a strategic agreement with Microsoft to deliver unified, AI-powered solutions that combine Informatica’s Intelligent Data Management Cloud platform with key Microsoft services, including Microsoft Fabric and Microsoft Azure.
  • Informatica’s cloud data management platform is available as a SaaS offering on Azure and via Azure Marketplace.
  • INFA +1.36% premarket to $19.7366.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Amaroq Minerals Ltd. reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Amaroq Minerals Ltd. press release (OTCPK:AMRQF): Q1 liquidity of C$23.4 million as at 31 March 2025.
  • Outlook for 2025We continue to target a run rate production of 300t/d in Q4 2025. During this commissioning phase and as a result of promising operational progress the company expects full year gold production to be in the range of 5 – 20koz.
  • This for example entails stopping operations while we install the final automated electrical systems, water system and the phase 2 flotation system. There is however, potential to narrow this guidance range as we progress operations through the remainder of the year.

data.title: Xenia Hotels declares $0.14 dividend
data.description:
• Xenia Hotels (NYSE:XHR) declares $0.14/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 4.55%
  • Payable July 15; for shareholders of record June 30; ex-div June 30.
  • See XHR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Omnicell opens innovation lab in Texas
data.description:
• Omnicell (NASDAQ:OMCL) announced on Wednesday the  opening of its Innovation Lab, located at 1005 East St. Elmo Road in Austin, Texas. 
  • The facility will serve as a hub focused on addressing challenges along the medication and supply journey. 
  • Omnicell (NASDAQ:OMCL) shares were up more than 3% in premarket trading.

data.title: Sanara MedTech GAAP EPS of -$0.41 beats by $0.10, revenue of $23.4M misses by $0.08M
data.description:
• Sanara MedTech press release (NASDAQ:SMTI): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.41 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $23.4M (+26.5% Y/Y) misses by $0.08M.

data.title: Nvidia, AMD 'top beneficiaries' from Saudi deals, but Broadcom and Marvell will benefit: BofA
data.description:
• While Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) are likely the biggest beneficiaries from yesterday's artificial intelligence deals with Saudi Arabia, Broadcom (AVGO) and Marvell (MRVL) should also benefit, Bank of America said.
  • “NVDA and AMD are likely to be main beneficiaries of the Saudi project, though we also highlight complementary networking benefits for AVGO, MRVL, optical, memory, semicap and foundry ecosystem,” analysts led by Vivek Arya wrote in a note to clients. “Details are still to come but based on press releases and industry discussions, we believe NVDA and AMD are working on separate projects, each with similar 500 Megawatts of projected AI compute capacity.” 
  • Arya, who raised his price targets on Nvidia and AMD to $160 and $130 from $150 and $120, respectively, said that even though Nvidia is getting direct awards and AMD is working on joint ventures, it's still an “important win” for the Dr. Lisa Su-led AMD.
  • The firm reiterated its Buy ratings on Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom and Marvell amid the belief that the AI opportunity will provide benefits long-term.

data.title: Spruce Power authorizes new share buyback program of up to $50M
data.description:
• Spruce Power (NYSE:SPRU) said its board has authorized a share repurchase program to buy back up to $50 million of its common stock on or before May 15, 2027.
  • Under the firm's previous share repurchase program, which commenced on May 15, 2023, through May 12, 2025, it repurchased an aggregate of 1.87M shares

data.title: KKR upgraded to Overweight at Morgan Stanley ahead of capital market recovery
data.description:
Morgan Stanley upgraded alternative asset manager KKR (NYSE:KKR) to Overweight from Equalweight, calling it a play on an anticipated capital markets recovery. KKR (NYSE:KKR) stock rose 2.0% in Wednesday premarket trading.

The greater-than-expected tariff de-escalation with China paves the way for a better-than-feared macro path with lower recession probability and reduced upward pressure on inflation, reducing tail risk, analyst Michael J. Cyprys said.

"Combined, these macro conditions can improve market confidence in a potential capital markets recovery and re-accelerate the private markets flywheel, supporting a robust earnings outlook for KKR," he wrote in a note to clients.

The shares' 15% year-to-date decline provides an "attractive" entry point, due to greater visibility on trade policy, as compared with the start of the year, "and with tail risks likely mitigated," Cyprys noted. 

Morgan Stanley raises its 2026 EPS estimate for KKR (KKR) by 4.5% and increases its price target to $150 from $120.

The Overweight rating contrasts with the SA Quant rating and the average SA Analyst rating, both at Hold, and aligns with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: Gorilla Technology names Bruce Bower as CFO
data.description:
• Gorilla Technology (NASDAQ:GRRR) said it has appointed Bruce Bower as its chief financial officer, effective 1 June.
  •  Bower had joined the firm as the interim CFO in September 2024.
